expectations.text: I thought maybe it'd be like, one person? Like a doctor lady who I'd see every time?
I thought maybe somebody would just like, tell me what to do about it? Like, take a pill or something?

perceptions.text: But it's been different people a lot. That's been... I don't know, kinda weird.
The lady at the front desk, she was nice.
They use all these big words and I just nod, but then I go home and I'm confused and worried

valued_items.text: There was this one nurse, I think? She was real nice. She didn't make me feel dumb when I didn't know what something was. She just... explained it different.
they did the scan thing right away, which was cool. I got to see the baby.

missing_items.text: nobody really asked me how I was feeling, you know? They just kept talking about the baby
Nobody really gave me anything written down that I could look at later or nothing.

service_gaps.expectation: Personalized, consistent relationship with one provider.
service_gaps.perception: Seeing different providers each visit, leading to a lack of familiarity.
service_gaps.innovation_potential: Assign a named midwife or primary lead professional for the duration of the pregnancy journey.

service_gaps.expectation: Clear, plain-language guidance on medical conditions and pregnancy steps.
service_gaps.perception: Information overload with complex terminology that is not retained or understood.
service_gaps.innovation_potential: Digital health summaries and 'plain English' care plans delivered via an accessible interface.

innovation_opportunities.source_quote: They use all these big words and I just nod, but then I go home and I'm confused

innovation_opportunities.description: Emotional check-in tools that prioritize patient well-being over clinical stats
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: nobody really asked me how I was feeling... they just kept talking about the baby

innovation_opportunities.description: SMS or app-based appointment 'pre-briefings' to clarify expectations before arrival
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: They don't call ahead much and be like 'hey, here's what's gonna happen.'

expectations.text: In China, you get pregnant, then... doctor. Just like that.
I think doctor say... eat good food. Baby good. Husband come, yes.

perceptions.text: Doctor... very fast. No time talk much.
The nurse... she fast. She not explain much.
Doctor talk... to me. Direct. Good surprise.

valued_items.text: One nurse... very kind. Smile. Show picture baby... She explain nice. Not rush.
Doctor... I trust more. Real person, yes?

missing_items.text: I need... someone explain slow. Someone... someone sit, talk, understand. Is lonely, understand?
Interpreter? No... no interpreter. Husband translate. Always husband.

service_gaps.expectation: Expectation of doctor explaining details clearly.
service_gaps.perception: Doctor speaks too fast using terminology like 'anterior' and 'percentile' without explanation.
service_gaps.innovation_potential: Visual-heavy educational materials and simplified summary sheets.

service_gaps.expectation: Expectation of a calm, accessible clinical environment.
service_gaps.perception: Crowded, loud clinic with 2-3 hour wait times.
service_gaps.innovation_potential: Digital appointment management or telehealth for routine follow-ups.

innovation_opportunities.description: Visual diagnostic report summaries that use infographics instead of complex text to explain ultrasound results.
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: Maybe... maybe picture explain more? With... with words?

innovation_opportunities.description: On-demand multilingual video interpretation or simplified audio summaries of consultations sent to the patient's phone.
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: She say many thing, I say 'yes, yes' but... nothing stay here. No one... no one slow down.

innovation_opportunities.description: Integration of 'cultural navigation' or social support screening to address the isolation of migrant mothers.
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: Family in China far far away. Here, no one... I need... someone sit, talk, understand.

expectations.text: I thought they'd, like, know what they're doing. Listen to you.
I guess I expected... someone would actually hear me. When I said something felt wrong, they'd take it seriously.
I thought they'd just tell me stuff. Like, the person doing the scan would say what they see, you know? And if something was wrong, they'd... they'd explain it.

perceptions.text: The doctor... when I told him I was scared about the baby, he just... he looked at his computer. Not at me. Just said 'the numbers look fine' and then the nurse was already calling the next person in.
The midwife was looking at the clock the whole time. I had questions about bleeding and pain, and she just said 'that's normal' and... done.
There was one nurse, actually. First one. She sat down. Like, actually sat. Asked me questions and waited for the answers.

valued_items.text: She sat down. Like, actually sat. Asked me questions and waited for the answers.
I needed someone to just... talk to me. Not at me. Like, 'Here's what we see, here's what it probably means, here's what we do next.'

missing_items.text: I didn't get any... I didn't know support groups existed. Nobody offered. Nobody asked if I was okay, you know?
No counseling. Nobody asked how I was doing mentally.
And written stuff? They didn't give me anything. Nothing to take home, nothing to read later.

service_gaps.expectation: Expected providers to listen and take concerns seriously.
service_gaps.perception: Providers looked at computers/clocks, dismissed concerns as 'normal', and moved on to next patients.

service_gaps.expectation: Clear explanation of scan results/next steps.
service_gaps.perception: Vague 'unclear' labels, no written information, 3-day wait for follow-up.
service_gaps.innovation_potential: Digital summary tools providing instant, layperson-friendly results.

innovation_opportunities.description: Patient-facing digital dashboard that explains scan results in simple terms and outlines the follow-up process immediately.
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: Simple. But instead it was all... medical words I didn't understand, and they were moving on to the next patient.

innovation_opportunities.description: Integrated mental health triage at every milestone, offering peer group referrals or counseling as a standard of care rather than reactive.
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: Nobody asked if I was okay, you know? Just... check the box, move to the next person.

expectations.text: I figured it’d be simple. Early scan, some blood taken, someone telling me what to expect and when to come back. Thought there’d be a real person in the room most times.
I expected more steady care, early scans, clear follow-up.

perceptions.text: Felt like I was shuttled between a doc who showed up once a month, a nurse on a spotty satellite, and whatever ER folks were on duty. Records didn’t follow me.
When the traveling doc actually showed up he sat down and listened. Didn’t feel rushed for once, and he let me ask things plain.

valued_items.text: One person who knew my whole story and stayed on it.

missing_items.text: A way to keep one consistent prenatal record when you see different providers.
Practical plans for folks who live far from hospitals — when to leave, what signs mean 'go now'.

service_gaps.expectation: Expectation of continuity of care and a single consistent medical record.
service_gaps.perception: Fragmented care across traveling OB, telehealth, and ER with no shared records.
service_gaps.innovation_potential: Unified cloud-based longitudinal health record accessible to rural clinics.

service_gaps.expectation: Reliable digital access to information and care via telehealth.
service_gaps.perception: Failed telehealth encounters due to poor satellite connectivity.
service_gaps.innovation_potential: Asynchronous/Offline-first health apps and store-and-forward telehealth tools.

innovation_opportunities.description: Create an offline-first 'pregnancy passport' digital tool that syncs when connection is available, ensuring providers can access the full history regardless of the specific clinic location.
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: Records didn’t follow me — nobody had the whole picture.

innovation_opportunities.description: Develop a localized rural pregnancy safety protocol, including clear 'go-now' signage and triggers for women living >90 miles from hospitals.
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: Practical plans for folks who live far from hospitals — when to leave, what signs mean 'go now'.

expectations.text: I thought it would be... easier? Like you tell the GP, they tell you what to do, set you up with a midwife, testing, boom. Simple.
I thought someone would sit me down and say it all slowly. I don’t know, a midwife or the sonographer or my GP. I wanted plain words.

perceptions.text: GP seemed rushed, like they wanted to tick boxes. I left with a paper and no real answers.
One nurse was nice and handed me a leaflet, but mostly it was quick and clinical.

valued_items.text: Helpful bit — the receptionist was nicer than anyone. Small thing, but it made me feel less nervous.
NHS for straight facts. Not fluffy.

missing_items.text: No one said, 'How are you really?' I wanted someone to say that once.
Real talk about messy life stuff... When to tell work? How to get help without drama.

service_gaps.expectation: Expected a structured, guided pathway where a professional explains the steps.
service_gaps.perception: Felt rushed through a 'tick-box' exercise with minimal explanation.
service_gaps.innovation_potential: Automated, personalized digital care roadmap to manage expectations and timeline.

service_gaps.expectation: Expected emotional support and a human, warm interaction.
service_gaps.perception: Experienced clinical, cold delivery where the patient felt dismissed.
service_gaps.innovation_potential: Integrated mental health triage or 'warm handover' protocols for GPs/midwives.

innovation_opportunities.description: Develop a 'Plain Language' digital companion tool that bridges the gap between clinical jargon and patient understanding.
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: I wanted plain words, not loads of medical talk.

innovation_opportunities.description: Implement an 'Emotional Check-in' prompt within the electronic health record system to ensure providers ask 'How are you?' during every consult.
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: No one said, 'How are you really?' I wanted someone to say that once.

innovation_opportunities.description: Create a standardized, non-medical 'Life During Pregnancy' guide that addresses social stressors like workplace disclosure and relationship dynamics.
innovation_opportunities.source_quote: I couldn't find easy info on privacy or options if you don't want family involved yet.
